engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Exiga Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Exiga in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for grey import specialists.

cards.altText: Subaru Exiga FB20 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Piston ring wear - causes excessive oil consumption on high-mileage grey imports after 70k miles.
Timing chain tensioner rattle - cold start noise on pre-2010 units if service intervals missed.
VVT solenoid wear - rough idle and warning lights on higher-mileage Japanese imports.

cards.altText: Subaru Exiga EJ20 engine - petrol turbo 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - turbo heat cycles cause MLS gasket degradation on high-mileage units.
Turbocharger wear - boost loss and oil consumption after 80k miles on grey imports.
Timing belt failure - interference engine risks valve damage if belt snaps after 60k miles.

cards.altText: Subaru Exiga EE20 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF clogging - short-journey driving causes blockages requiring expensive regeneration or replacement.
EGR valve failure - carbon buildup causes hesitation and emissions test failures on grey imports.
Timing chain stretch - high-mileage units develop rattle if oil changes delayed.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £3,200-£4,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EE20 units - actual quotes may vary.

VVT solenoid wear triggering rough idle and check engine lights on Japanese imports

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Exiga Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Exiga relies primarily on the FB20 naturally aspirated petrol boxer across most UK grey import variants, with EJ20 turbo and rare EE20 diesel options. While generally robust, oil consumption from piston ring wear, timing chain tensioner rattle and CVT compatibility concerns dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: FB20 Oil Consumption - Why Subaru Exiga Engines Burn Oil Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Exiga 2.0i, Crossover 7, S Package (2008-2018, FB20/FB20A)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-100,000 miles on grey imports with incomplete service history
problems.rootCause: Early FB20 piston ring design allows oil to bypass into combustion chamber; worsened by Japanese short-trip driving patterns and infrequent oil changes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gaskets, monitor oil consumption
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary reduction; does not address root piston ring issue

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt FB20 with Upgraded Rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB20 with revised piston rings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; addresses root cause with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On a grey import Exiga worth £4,000-£8,000, if oil consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles, replacement with a rebuilt FB20 featuring upgraded rings at £2,200-£3,800 offers better long-term value than repeated top-ups and risk of catalytic converter damage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FB20 engine prices for your Subaru Exiga

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Rattle - Cold Start Noise on Subaru Exiga FB20
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if oil changes delayed or incorrect viscosity used
problems.rootCause: Early FB20 tensioner design loses pressure on cold start; rattle indicates chain slap that can damage guides if ignored.

repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; prevents secondary damage to sprockets

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt FB20 with Revised Tensioner
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB20 with updated tensioner, full timing service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattle accompanied by performance loss or high mileage

problems.recommendation: If your Exiga shows only cold-start rattle with no performance loss, tensioner replacement at £420-£750 is sensible. However, on grey imports over 80k miles, upgrading to a rebuilt FB20 with revised components often proves better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for timing-chain-upgraded Subaru Exiga engine replacement

problems.h4: Lineartronic CVT Compatibility - Judder & Adaptation Issues on Subaru Exiga
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Exiga 2.0i, Crossover 7 with Lineartronic CVT (2008-2018)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage if replacement engine lacks CVT-specific calibration
problems.rootCause: CVT flexplate and ECU adaptation must match donor engine; mismatch causes judder, warning lights, or transmission damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: ECU reset, flexplate inspection, CVT fluid service, road test adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth operation if engine internals are sound and compatible

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB20 with CVT-compatible flexplate, ECU adaptation included, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed compatibility; essential for grey import CVT Exiga owners

problems.recommendation: For CVT-equipped Exiga owners, never fit a non-CVT-verified engine. A rebuilt FB20 with CVT-compatible components at £2,400-£4,000 prevents costly transmission damage and ensures smooth operation post-installation.
problems.cta: Find CVT-compatible Subaru Exiga engine replacements

problems.h4: EJ20 Head Gasket Failure - Turbo Heat Stress on Subaru Exiga 2.0GT
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles on enthusiast-driven or poorly maintained grey imports
problems.rootCause: Turbo heat cycles stress multi-layer steel gasket; failure causes coolant/oil mixing, overheating, potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, fit upgraded MLS gasket, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head undamaged; requires specialist Subaru knowledge

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EJ20 with Upgraded Gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EJ20 with upgraded MLS gasket, turbo inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating occurred or mileage exceeds 90k; peace of mind for turbo owners

problems.recommendation: If your 2.0GT shows only minor seepage with no overheating history, gasket replacement at £750-£1,400 may suffice. However, if overheating occurred or mileage is high, a rebuilt EJ20 at £2,800-£4,200 avoids repeat failures and protects your investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ20 turbo engine prices for your Subaru Exiga

problems.h4: EE20 DPF & EGR Issues - Emissions Compliance on Subaru Exiga Diesel
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles on short-journey UK driving patterns
problems.rootCause: Boxer diesel DPF requires sustained high-speed driving to regenerate; UK urban use causes clogging, EGR carbon buildup, MOT failures.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF clean, EGR valve replacement, ECU adaptation, emissions test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores MOT compliance if engine internals sound; requires ongoing motorway use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EE20 with UK-spec emissions components, full documentation, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed MOT pass; ideal for grey import diesel Exiga owners seeking compliance certainty

problems.recommendation: For diesel Exiga owners facing MOT failure, a rebuilt EE20 with UK-spec emissions components at £3,200-£4,500 provides peace of mind and avoids repeated testing costs, especially given the rarity of EE20 parts in the UK.
problems.cta: Compare MOT-compliant Subaru Exiga diesel engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Exiga
finalCta.paragraph: The Subaru Exiga's boxer engines are robust when maintained, but grey import status, age and niche parts availability mean failures can be costly. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand Exiga compatibility, chassis codes (YA/YB), CVT adaptation and MOT compliance. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your Exiga back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Exiga Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Exiga and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru Exiga Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Subaru Exiga is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes for UK grey import owners.

items.title: Subaru Exiga 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L FB20 naturally aspirated petrol boxer engine powered most Subaru Exiga variants sold as grey imports to the UK from 2008-2018. This aluminium-block DOHC unit with AVCS variable valve timing delivered 148hp with strong reliability when maintained, though early units require attention to piston ring design and timing chain tensioner service intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption
timing chain tensioner rattle on cold starts
VVT solenoid wear on high-mileage imports
items.cta: Compare Subaru Exiga 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Exiga 2.0L Turbo Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L EJ20 turbocharged boxer engine offered high-performance motivation for Exiga 2.0GT variants, delivering 280hp with the characteristic Subaru turbo surge. Legacy-derived design with DOHC and turbocharging requires diligent maintenance of head gaskets, turbo components and timing belts to avoid costly failures on grey imports.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure from turbo heat cycles
timing belt failure risk on interference design
items.cta: Compare Subaru Exiga 2.0L turbo petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Exiga 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L EE20 horizontally-opposed diesel engine was Subaru's unique boxer diesel offering, fitted to rare Exiga 2.0D grey imports. While efficient, the EE20 requires sustained high-speed driving for DPF regeneration and is sensitive to service intervals; UK urban use patterns can accelerate emissions system failures.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain stretch on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Subaru Exiga 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Subaru Exiga Petrol Engines
items.description: The Subaru Exiga was offered predominantly with FB20 naturally aspirated and EJ20 turbocharged 2.0L petrol boxer engines across all UK grey import variants from 2008-2018.
items.descriptor: The Subaru Exiga was offered predominantly with FB20 naturally aspirated and EJ20 turbocharged 2.0L petrol boxer engines across all UK grey import variants from 2008-2018.

families: FB20 - dominant naturally aspirated unit (2.0i, Crossover 7), oil consumption concerns on early revisions
EJ20 - turbocharged performance option (2.0GT), head gasket and turbo wear considerations
foundIn: First Generation YA Chassis (2008-2012) - FB20, EJ20 across all petrol trims
Facelift YB Chassis/Crossover 7 (2013-2018) - Revised FB20A/B, updated EJ20 calibration
knownFor: Horizontally-opposed boxer design delivers low centre of gravity and smooth power delivery
FB20 piston ring wear causing oil consumption on high-mileage grey imports after 70k miles
EJ20 turbo heat cycles stress head gaskets; timing belt replacement critical every 60k miles
Strong cross-reference parts availability via shared Subaru platform components

engineYears.h2: Subaru Exiga Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Exiga production timeline, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their FB20/EJ20 variant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Subaru Exiga launched in Japan with YA chassis codes, introducing the FB20 naturally aspirated and EJ20 turbo boxer engines across FWD and AWD variants.
keyChanges: First production year for Subaru Exiga grey imports to UK
YA chassis codes introduced for all variants with FB20/EJ20 engine options
Early FB20 calibration with pre-revision piston rings requiring attention on high-mileage units
mainEngines: FB20 - 2.0L petrol naturally aspirated (2.0i, S Package)
EE20 - 2.0L diesel boxer (2.0D, rare UK imports)
knownFor: Early FB20 units may exhibit oil consumption; verify piston ring revision status
EJ20 turbo head gasket vigilance essential on enthusiast-driven examples
Strong donor parts availability via shared Subaru Legacy/Outback platform
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008 Subaru Exiga replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru Exiga 2008 engine replacement • YA chassis FB20 compatibility • Early FB20 oil consumption fix

years.description: Mid-production FB20A revision introduced improved piston rings to address oil consumption concerns on early YA chassis Exiga units.
keyChanges: FB20A engine code denotes revised piston ring design for reduced oil consumption
Updated timing chain tensioner (part 13021AA000) addresses cold-start rattle on early units
Final YA chassis production with refined emissions calibration for Japanese standards
mainEngines: FB20A - 2.0L petrol with revised rings (2.0i late YA)
EJ20 - 2.0L turbo with updated turbocharger seals (2.0GT)
knownFor: FB20A units show significantly reduced oil consumption vs early FB20
Timing chain service remains critical; revised tensioner improves cold-start behaviour
Grey import documentation must confirm engine code revision for accurate replacement matching
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010 Subaru Exiga replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru Exiga FB20A oil consumption fix • YA chassis timing chain upgrade • Revised piston ring verification
enquiries: Subaru Exiga FB20A oil consumption fix

years.description: The 2013 facelift introduced YB chassis codes and Crossover 7 styling with updated FB20B calibration, essential for UK MOT compliance on late grey imports.
keyChanges: YB chassis codes replaced YA variants with revised mounting points and emissions systems
FB20B engine code denotes final production revision with further reliability refinements
Crossover 7 aesthetic package added without mechanical changes to FB20/EJ20 core architecture
mainEngines: EJ20 - 2.0L turbo with updated ECU mapping (2.0GT Crossover 7)
knownFor: Updated emissions calibration simplifies UK MOT and IVA test compliance on late imports
FB20B units represent lowest-risk naturally aspirated option for grey import Exiga buyers
Final production year 2018 units are rarest and most sought-after by collectors
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013 Subaru Exiga replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru Exiga Crossover 7 engine replacement • YB chassis FB20B compatibility • Late-model emissions certification

faq.h2: Subaru Exiga Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Exiga engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Exiga engine replacement costs range from £1,800 for Japanese used imports to £4,500 for UK reconditioned diesel units, depending on engine code, variant and supplier. Supply & fit services add £550-£950 labour.
keyPoints: Japanese used FB20 engines start from £1,800 with auction grade verification
UK reconditioned FB20 units range £2,200-£3,800 with 12-month warranty
EJ20 turbo and EE20 diesel variants typically cost £400-£800 more than FB20
Supply & fit labour adds £550-£950 depending on specialist location and CVT/AWD complexity
items.cta: Compare Subaru Exiga engine replacement quotes from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What engine code fits my Subaru Exiga?
items.answer: Most Subaru Exiga variants use the FB20 2.0L naturally aspirated petrol boxer engine code, but chassis codes YA (2008-2012) and YB (2013-2018) determine compatibility with your vehicle's mounting points and emissions systems.
keyPoints: FB20 is the dominant engine code across 2.0i, Crossover 7 and S Package variants
Verify chassis code YA for pre-facelift or YB for Crossover 7 compatibility before ordering
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code confirmation
EJ20 turbo and rare EE20 diesel codes require specialist verification for correct replacement matching
items.cta: Verify your Subaru Exiga FB20 engine code instantly via DVLA lookup

items.question: Is a reconditioned Subaru Exiga engine better than a used Japanese import?
items.answer: Reconditioned FB20 engines offer UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing known failures like piston ring wear, while used Japanese imports provide lower upfront cost but limited warranty and potential compliance documentation gaps.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include 12-month UK warranty vs 3-6 months for Japanese imports
Rebuilt FB20 engines feature revised piston rings addressing Exiga oil consumption patterns
Japanese imports require additional IVA/MOT documentation verification for UK compliance
Budget-conscious owners may prefer imports; long-term owners benefit from reconditioned reliability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Subaru Exiga engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Subaru Exiga UK MOT or registration?
items.answer: Replacement engines must meet UK emissions standards and include proper documentation for DVLA registration; EngineMarket specialists supply FB20/EJ20 units with Euro 4 certification and IVA support for grey import compliance.
keyPoints: All replacement FB20 engines include UK-spec catalytic converter for MOT emissions compliance
Emissions certification documentation provided for DVLA registration updates on grey imports
IVA test support included for Exiga vehicles requiring Individual Vehicle Approval
Specialists handle ECU immobiliser adaptation and CVT flexplate calibration for seamless UK registration transfer
items.cta: Find MOT-compliant Subaru Exiga engine replacements with full documentation

items.question: How long does Subaru Exiga engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: UK reconditioned FB20 engines typically dispatch within 2 days; Japanese imports require 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day fitting. Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours for in-stock units.
keyPoints: In-stock UK reconditioned units: 2-day supply & fit turnaround typical for FB20 variants
Japanese import engines: 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day specialist fitting with chassis verification
CVT and AWD variants may require additional time for adaptation service and drivetrain compatibility checks
Emergency replacement helpline available for seized engine scenarios with priority dispatch from UK stock
items.cta: Check live Subaru Exiga engine stock availability and delivery timelines

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru Exiga engine myself to save money?
items.answer: DIY engine fitting is not recommended for Subaru Exiga grey imports due to boxer engine-specific tools, CVT adaptation requirements, AWD drivetrain calibration and UK compliance documentation best handled by specialists.
keyPoints: FB20/EJ20 boxer engines require specific lifting equipment and timing tools for safe installation
CVT variants need flexplate calibration and ECU adaptation service - specialist tooling essential
IVA/MOT documentation support requires specialist knowledge of grey import compliance procedures
Supply & fit packages often cost less than DIY when factoring tool hire, risk of errors and warranty validity
items.cta: Book Subaru Exiga engine supply & fit with grey import specialist network

items.question: What are the most common Subaru Exiga engine problems?
items.answer: FB20 piston ring wear causing oil consumption, timing chain tensioner rattle on cold starts, and Lineartronic CVT compatibility issues dominate Subaru Exiga replacement enquiries from UK grey import owners.
keyPoints: FB20 oil consumption affects early revisions; rebuilt units include upgraded piston rings as standard
Timing chain tensioner rattle on pre-2010 FB20 units requires revised part 13021AA000 for permanent fix
CVT-equipped Exiga require flexplate and ECU adaptation verification to prevent judder post-installation
All rebuilt FB20 units include upgraded components addressing these known grey import failure points
items.cta: Compare Subaru Exiga engine replacements with failure-proof upgraded components

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing my Subaru Exiga engine?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 60% of a reconditioned FB20 price (£2,200-£3,800), replacement offers better long-term value with UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing original failure patterns.
keyPoints: Minor repairs (PCV refresh, tensioner replacement) cost £280-£750 and may be viable for low-mileage units
Major failures (oil consumption, overheating damage) typically require full replacement for reliability
Rebuilt FB20 engines include 12-month warranty vs limited coverage on repaired original units
Consider vehicle value: Exiga grey imports typically worth £4,000-£8,000 in UK market
items.cta: Get instant repair vs replace cost comparison for your Subaru Exiga variant

items.question: Does the Subaru Exiga engine work with other Subaru donor parts?
items.answer: Yes - the FB20 engine was also fitted to Subaru XV, Forester and Legacy in other markets, expanding UK parts availability and reducing lead times for grey import Exiga owners seeking replacement components.
keyPoints: FB20 cross-compatibility increases donor pool for hard-to-find Exiga-specific components
Timing kits, gaskets and sensors often available from Subaru XV/Forester suppliers in UK
Verify part numbers match your Exiga chassis code (YA/YB) and variant before ordering
EngineMarket specialists source from cross-platform Subaru inventory to reduce wait times
items.cta: Find cross-compatible Subaru Exiga FB20 parts via XV/Forester/Legacy donor network
